name: Project Charter to WBS and WBS to Jira user story - testing

values.content:
You are a Senior Business Analyst.
From the given project scope:
1. Generate a Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) grouped strictly by User Story.
2. Create EXACTLY ONE JSON object per User Story.
3. Do NOT mix or reuse Acceptance Criteria or Sub-tasks across different User Stories.

🔒 Mandatory Rules (VERY IMPORTANT)
Before generating project-specific items, you MUST ALWAYS include the following standard modules and epics for every project, even if they are not explicitly mentioned in the scope.

Module/Subject: Technical Planning & Setup
Epic/Module: High-level – Technical Planning
You MUST generate the following User Stories. Each User Story MUST start with “As a …” and MUST NOT include numbering like “User Story 1.1”.
* As a project stakeholder, I want to decide the development team structure and technology stack so that the project is scalable, maintainable, and aligned with business goals.
* As a technical architect, I want to create a high-level application architecture diagram so that all stakeholders understand system components and integrations.
* As a technical lead, I want to identify challenging areas and define a POC/R&D plan so that technical risks are addressed early.
* As a project manager, I want to define an internal and client communication plan so that coordination and reporting are clear and consistent.

Epic/Module: Project Setup
User Stories must follow the “As a … I want … so that …” format.
* As a development team, I want to define routing and branch naming conventions so that code management remains consistent and traceable.
* As a DevOps engineer, I want to define a deployment plan so that releases across environments are predictable and controlled. Sub-tasks:
    * Plan around the need for different environments (dev, staging, live)
* As a developer, I want to set up the development environment so that coding can begin smoothly. Sub-tasks:
    * Create required Bitbucket repositories
* As a QA lead, I want to define a testing strategy so that quality is ensured throughout development. Sub-tasks:
    * Create a module-level development and testing plan with timelines

Epic/Module: Database Design
* As a database architect, I want to plan the database structure so that data integrity, performance, and scalability are ensured. Sub-tasks:
    * Create ER diagram
    * Define master tables
    * Define indexes, unique keys, and foreign keys
* As a backend developer, I want to manage database migrations in the project so that schema changes are versioned and traceable. Sub-tasks:
    * Create database migrations/schema in the project

Last Module/Subject: Deployment(last module show after end module)
Epic/Module: Deployment
* As a DevOps engineer, I want to configure deployment so that the system is securely and reliably accessible in production. Sub-tasks:(detect from project tracker that its webapp or mobile app or both, also based on front-end and backend deployment)

📌 Project-Specific Scope Handling
After adding the mandatory modules above:
* Generate additional User Stories strictly based on the provided project scope.
* Ensure every User Story starts with “As a …”
* Ensure each User Story has its own Acceptance Criteria and Sub-tasks.
* Do NOT merge Sub-tasks between User Stories.

📋 For EACH User Story:
* Generate 5–7 clear Acceptance Criteria
* List ONLY the Sub-tasks that belong to that specific User Story
* Create subtasks based on roles like frontend, backend...

📦 Output Format (STRICT)
Return STRICT JSON in the following format ONLY:

[
  {
    "Module/Subject": "",
    "Epic/Module": "",
    "User Story/Task": "",
    "Acceptance Criteria": [
      "AC 1",
      "AC 2"
    ],
    "Sub-tasks": [
      "Subtask 1",
      "Subtask 2"
    ]
  }
]

🚫 Output Rules (STRICT)
* - No markdown
* - No explanations
* - No additional keys
* * - No merging of subtasks between User Stories

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "key": "{{ $('Prepare Jira Project Data').item.json.projectKey }}",
  "name": "{{ $('Prepare Jira Project Data').item.json.projectName }}",
  "projectTypeKey": "software",
  "projectTemplateKey": "com.pyxis.greenhopper.jira:gh-scrum-template",
  "leadAccountId": "{{ $('Prepare Jira Project Data').item.json.leadAccountId }}",
  "assigneeType": "UNASSIGNED"
}

nodes.name: Save Project Key for Later Use

values.content:
=You are an expert diagrams.net (draw.io) architect specializing in Database ERD Design.

From the provided document, generate a VALID draw.io mxfile XML.

ERD Architecture Rules:
1. Use ONLY the "Entity Relation" table shape (style="shape=table;childLayout=tableLayout;").
2. Create a distinct Header Row for each Table Name.
3. Organize fields into separate rows. Clearly label Primary Keys (PK) and Foreign Keys (FK).
4. Use "Orthogonal" connectors for relationships (style=orthogonalEdgeStyle) so lines don't cross through tables.
5. Manually assign (x, y) coordinates to ensure tables are spread apart by at least 200px.

Safety & Encoding (CRITICAL):
1. Do NOT use raw '<' or '>' symbols inside attribute values. Use HTML entities if needed (e.g., < instead of <).
2. For enum types or descriptions, use curly braces { } instead of angle brackets < >.
3. Ensure every <mxCell> has a unique numeric ID.

Output Constraints:
1. Output ONLY raw XML starting with <mxfile and ending with </mxfile>.
2. No markdown, no code blocks, and no preamble.

Document:
{{ $json.content }}

json.User Story/Task: User Story/Task
json.Acceptance Criteria: Acceptance Criteria
json.Sub-tasks: Sub-tasks

json.User Story/Task: As an AI agent, I want to automatically process and respond to email-based rental enquiries so that we can streamline the client inquiry workflow.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• System can receive forwarded emails to a designated email address.
• Emails are scanned for defined keywords in both subject and body.
• Relevant enquiries are identified and processed automatically.
• System supports both direct email and website-generated enquiries.
• A summary of the matched criteria is generated for each incoming enquiry.
• Incorrectly formatted or irrelevant emails are identified and flagged.
• Enquiries for 5 days or more are flagged for manual handover to client.
json.Sub-tasks: Integrate with SMTP and designated client email inbox.
Define and configure enquiry keywords list.
Develop email parsing and summary logic.
Implement email-to-lead recognition flow.
Set up workflow for automated vs. handover pathway.
Create logging for processed and skipped emails.

json.User Story/Task: As a system, I need to maintain master data for products, inventory, and delivery so the AI can generate accurate proposals.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• All primary products and priced accessories are stored in a master list.
• Inventory management assumes unlimited stock of radios and accessories.
• Accessory prompts are configured for earpieces, surveillance kits, and microphones only.
• Metal detector upsell logic is set up in the master.
• Delivery master is created for NSW, Queensland, and VIC locations.
• Batteries and chargers are excluded from AI proposals.
• Accessory quantities match the radio count.
json.Sub-tasks: Input product and accessory data into the master database.
Configure master accessory prompt logic.
Develop metal detector upsell integration.
Set up delivery charge matrix for key states.
Exclude batteries/chargers from proposal generator.

json.User Story/Task: As an AI agent, I need to gather required details (dates, accessories, product counts) from new leads so I can determine package requirements.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• AI prompts for number of radios and accessories as required.
• AI asks for start date, end date, pickup date, and drop date if not specified.
• AI confirms accessory types and quantities directly with the client.
• Accessory quantity defaults to radio quantity if not specified.
• Metal detector upsell occurs during requirement gathering.
• Missing information is detected and re-prompted.
• Weekend units and event packages are excluded by default.
json.Sub-tasks: Design interactive prompt flows for gathering requirement details.
Develop conditional logic for missing information prompts.
Configure auto-defaults for accessory and radio quantities.
Add logic for metal detector upsell trigger.
Document requirements collection for logging and audit.

json.User Story/Task: As an AI agent, I need to generate accurate price proposals for new and returning customers as per defined business rules.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• System checks company name to identify returning customers via HirePOS.
• Previous order details are retrieved for returning clients.
• ±10% unit variance is calculated and last negotiated price is offered if within threshold.
• Base price negotiation starts for new clients or out-of-threshold orders.
• Only consumable/rental days are considered for price calculation.
• Quote format conforms to provided template/sample.
• No information is written back to HirePOS.
json.Sub-tasks: Integrate with HirePOS to fetch order history (read-only).
Implement business logic for price comparison and thresholds.
Develop pricing calculation module (including days logic).
Establish format for proposal email (body and attachment logic).
Enforce out-of-scope item exclusion.

json.User Story/Task: As an AI agent, I want to negotiate prices within predefined boundaries, escalating or declining as appropriate.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• AI can handle price negotiation with lower price requests.
• Negotiation is capped at last allowed price (handover threshold).
• AI politely declines requests below handover price.
• Leads exceeding boundaries are flagged for client handover.
• Negotiation history is logged for each enquiry.
• Customer handover occurs smoothly when triggered.
json.Sub-tasks: Develop negotiation dialogue flows.
Implement last price and handover price checks.
Log negotiation iterations and outcomes.
Integrate client notification for handovers.
Document decline messaging templates.

json.User Story/Task: As an AI agent, I need to request and process delivery details and charges based on the customer’s location as part of proposal generation.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• For NSW customers, no delivery fee unless requested.
• For all other eligible states, delivery/pickup charges are added as line items.
• AI collects basic customer details for new leads only.
• Customer details include company name, address, postal code, first and last name, and phone.
• Delivery costs are shown as separate from pickup, if both apply.
• Estimate is provided for all expected charges.
• Delivery for unlisted states is deferred to client input.
json.Sub-tasks: Prompt new clients for required details.
Auto-calculate delivery and pickup charges based on location.
Add logic to insert line items in proposal.
Exclude delivery charges for NSW self-pickup unless requested.
Validate state eligibility and handle exceptions.

json.User Story/Task: As an AI agent, I want to confirm and handover leads to the client under certain conditions to ensure proper escalation.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• Lead is handed over if duration exceeds 5 days.
• Lead is handed over if requested price is below last negotiated price.
• Client receives full context via email or notification.
• AI seeks final confirmation from the lead before handover.
• All handovers are logged for traceability.
• No further actions are automated post-handover.
json.Sub-tasks: Define escalation and handover triggers.
Configure notification/email for client with lead context.
Add logging for all handovers.
Implement confirmation prompt with the lead.
Disable further AI workflow for handed over leads.

json.User Story/Task: As a system, I need to log all enquiry, lead, and sales discussion data in Google Sheets for auditing and tracking.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• All enquiry interactions are captured in a structured Google Sheet.
• Each row maintains lead email, name, date/time, company, inquiry details, event name (optional), quantity, estimated value, and sales stage.
• Sales status is updated on each relevant workflow stage.
• No confidential or excessive personal data is logged.
• Google Sheet matches the agreed format and fields with client.
• All status changes and negotiations are appended.
json.Sub-tasks: Integrate Google Sheets API for data logging.
Configure mapped fields based on workflow outputs.
Establish data update triggers at each workflow milestone.
Validate data structure adheres to client template.
Test sheet permissions and access.

rows.User Story/Task: As an AI agent, I want to maintain and utilize a master data set of products and delivery charges, so that accurate options and pricing are referenced during each inquiry.
rows.Acceptance Criteria:
• Product master includes two-way radios and all priced accessories (earpieces, surveillance kits, speaker microphones, metal detectors).
• Accessory quantities are auto-matched to the number of radios requested.
• AI prompts only for priced accessories, not batteries or chargers.
• Metal detectors are recommended as upsell.
• Delivery master stores delivery charges for NSW, Queensland, and VIC.
• Products and accessories data are kept up-to-date in the master set.
• Master data can be referenced by other system modules (e.g., pricing, proposal generation).
rows.Sub-tasks: 1. Compile complete list of products and add-ons.

fields.summary: As an AI agent, I want to automatically receive and identify rental inquiry emails from a dedicated forwarded account, so inquiries can be processed without manual intervention.

content.text:
• AI receives forwarded inquiry emails on a dedicated email address.
• AI scans email summary and body for defined keywords associated with rental inquiries.
• AI routes or flags relevant inquiries for automated processing.
• AI workflow is set up to analyze the email content and trigger appropriate actions.
• Sample and reference inquiries are used to validate filtering logic.
• Emails from both website and email-based forms are successfully detected.
• Email identifier, locator and handover details are configured as per client’s specification.

subtasks: 1. Configure SMTP and access credentials for new email account.
1. Validate detection of web form and manual inquiries.
1. Configure SMTP and access credentials for new email account.
1. Validate detection of web form and manual inquiries.

json.User Story/Task: As a project stakeholder, I want to decide the development team structure and technology stack so that the project is scalable, maintainable, and aligned with business goals.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• Development roles and responsibilities are defined
• A suitable technology stack is proposed and justified
• Stakeholders formally approve the proposed stack
• Technology stack aligns with scalability and maintainability requirements
• Tech stack is compatible with secure web-access
json.Sub-tasks: Define key team roles for project delivery

json.Acceptance Criteria:
• Architecture diagram includes all major system components
• Authentication method (email OTP) is clearly represented
• Data flows between modules are illustrated
• External and internal integrations are depicted
• Diagram is reviewed and approved by stakeholders
json.Sub-tasks: Draft initial architecture diagram

json.User Story/Task: As a technical lead, I want to identify challenging areas and define a POC/R&D plan so that technical risks are addressed early.

json.Sub-tasks: Identify challenging technical areas

json.User Story/Task: As a project manager, I want to define an internal and client communication plan so that coordination and reporting are clear and consistent.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• Communication plan includes both internal and client-facing processes
• Reporting frequency and ownership are clear
• Communication channels are specified
• Stakeholders acknowledge the plan
• Plan includes escalation matrix
json.Sub-tasks: Finalize communication document

json.User Story/Task: As a development team, I want to define routing and branch naming conventions so that code management remains consistent and traceable.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• Routing conventions are documented
• Branch naming conventions are defined
• Team is trained on conventions
• Conventions are accessible in shared documentation
• All repos adopt new conventions
json.Sub-tasks: Define routing conventions

json.User Story/Task: As a development team, I want to define routing and branch naming conventions so that code management remains consistent and traceable.

json.User Story/Task: As a DevOps engineer, I want to define a deployment plan so that releases across environments are predictable and controlled.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• Environments for dev, staging, and production are specified
• Deployment steps are clearly outlined
• Rollback strategy is included
• Access management for each environment is defined
• Deployment plan is reviewed by lead developer
json.Sub-tasks: Document deployment processes for each environment

json.Acceptance Criteria:

• Master tables for users, clients, reports, payments, etc. are defined
• Indexes and keys are implemented for critical queries
• Structure reviewed for scalability and performance
• Documentation reviewed by technical lead
json.Sub-tasks: Create ER diagram

json.User Story/Task: As a user (CEO or team member), I want to log in using an email-based OTP system so that my access is secure and I can use the app anywhere.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• Only CEO and specified team member have login access
• Login uses OTP sent to email address
• OTP validity is time-limited and secure
• Login works on web and mobile browsers
• Login session expiry is implemented
• Failed attempts are properly managed
• All login attempts are auditable
json.Sub-tasks: Implement email OTP generation and delivery

json.User Story/Task: As the CEO, I want to add or edit client details so that new clients can be onboarded and managed from the system.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• Add/edit functionality visible only to CEO after login
• Client details include name, email, location, and project name
• Client entry and edit forms validate all mandatory fields
• Successfully added/edited clients appear in client list
• Each client is uniquely identified
• No duplicate clients allowed
• Audit log is updated for client changes
json.Sub-tasks: Build client add/edit form

json.User Story/Task: As the CEO, I want to add or edit client details so that new clients can be onboarded and managed from the system.

json.User Story/Task: As the CEO, I want to receive email notifications for monthly reports and approve or reject them in the system so that only reviewed reports are sent to the client.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• CEO receives email notification for each report submission
• CEO can view, approve, or reject in the web panel
• Approval sends report to client via email
• Rejection allows CEO to enter reason and notifies user
• Rejection enables user to upload a revised report
• Report status updates correctly after action
• System tracks audit log for all approval actions
json.Sub-tasks: Build admin (CEO) review/approval panel

json.User Story/Task: As the system, I want to automatically notify the CEO and user when a payment receipt is not uploaded by the due date so that pending payments have visibility and prompt action.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• System tracks due date for each payment receipt
• Automatic email sent to CEO and user if receipt is pending
• Pending payment status visible in dashboard
• Notification email content includes client/project details
• Notifications are logged
• Due dates are configurable at project start
json.Sub-tasks: Configure pending payment dashboard panel

json.User Story/Task: As the system, I want to automatically notify the CEO and user when a payment receipt is not uploaded by the due date so that pending payments have visibility and prompt action.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• System tracks due date for each payment receipt
• Automatic email sent to CEO and user if receipt is pending
• Pending payment status visible in dashboard
• Notification email content includes client/project details
• Notifications are logged
• Due dates are configurable at project start
json.Sub-tasks: Allow due date configuration

json.User Story/Task: As the CEO, I want to view a dashboard showing the status of reports and payments so that I have clear and immediate visibility on project and payment status.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• Dashboard shows pending, approved, and sent reports
• Dashboard displays payments received and pending
• Data is real-time or near real-time
• Filter by client/project and time period is available
• Mobile and desktop access supported
• Statuses are visually distinct
• Dashboard does not require manual refresh for updates
json.Sub-tasks: Develop filter functionality

json.User Story/Task: As the CEO, I want to view a dashboard showing the status of reports and payments so that I have clear and immediate visibility on project and payment status.

json.User Story/Task: As a DevOps engineer, I want to configure deployment so that the system is securely and reliably accessible in production.
json.Acceptance Criteria:
• Production deployment is stable and accessible via web and mobile browsers
• Deployment uses HTTPS for security
• Environment variables and secrets are managed
• Availability monitoring is in place
• Rollback/restore procedures are documented
json.Sub-tasks: Set up production server/environment with HTTPS

json.Sub-tasks: Implement monitoring tools
